OpenAI builds first chip with Broadcom and TSMC, scales back foundry ambition OpenAI is working with Broadcom and TSMC to build its first in-house chip designed to support its artificial intelligence systems, while adding AMD chips alongside Nvidia chips to meet its surging infrastructure demands, sources told Reuters.  OpenAI, the fast-growing company behind ChatGPT, has examined a range of options to diversify chip supply and reduce costs.  OpenAI considered building everything in-house and raising capital for an expensive plan to build a network of factories known as foundries for chip manufacturing.

Harris defends CHIPS Act ahead of election, while Trump criticizes it The CHIPS and Science Act was passed with bipartisan support in August 2022 as a means to jumpstart and bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing within the US. Ahead of the 2024 Election Day on Tuesday, Vice President Kamala Harris defended the investments made into American chipmaking and the broader domestic industrial sector. Yahoo Finance special reporter Akiko Fujita highlights the political tensions arising around future of the CHIPS Act following comments made by House Speaker Mike Johnson (R) and former President and Republican candidate Donald Trump, as well as the potential impacts the US election results could have on major semiconductor players.
